Freshness comes from Cache-Control max-age, then (tick 347) the Expires header (the absolute-date signal CDNs/static assets still send instead of max-age). A stale entry carrying an ETag/Last-Modified is kept (not dropped) and the next GET rides If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since; a 304 reuses the stored body with no re-download and the 304's max-age refreshes freshness. manuk-net crate tests (in the verify wall): revalidates_stale_entry_with_etag_and_serves_304 + no_cache_with_last_modified_revalidates_via_if_modified_since · tick 348: the Age header (CDN-reported prior cache time) is subtracted from the freshness lifetime (RFC 7234 4.2.3), so a shared-cache-aged response is not treated as newer than the origin considers it.

what_breaks_without_it: the pre-standards web; a huge fraction of the long tail
receipt: tick 242 WIRED the dead-end: html5ever's verdict now rides on Dom::quirks (a field, so NO signature changes anywhere — every consumer already receives a &Dom, incl. all 18 cascade_styles call sites). Gate asserts RENDERING and REPORTING agree in both directions plus that the modes actually differ (WPT quirks/unitless-length: width:100 => 100px in quirks, dropped in standards). LimitedQuirks folds to false (it does not enable the unitless quirk). Full page suite re-run: 122 passed / 1 pre-existing hard_wall failure, no regressions from doctype-less fixtures becoming quirks. tick 243 COMPLETED it: all remaining MatchingContext/media-query sites now read qm_of(el.dom), AND — the trap — our own RuleIndex by_id/by_class buckets are keyed through the same index_key(v,qm), because telling the matcher 'case-insensitive' while the index keys by exact case discards the rule BEFORE matching (proven: reverting index_key alone makes #FOO give 800px not 250px). So `#FOO` matches id=foo in quirks and not in standards. ⚠ GATE NOT IN THE VERIFY WALL — see docs/loop/GATE-COVERAGE.md

what_breaks_without_it: GitHub 1k-5k-line blobs & long PR threads stutter
receipt: MEASURED tick 241 (was 'no node budget'): the verify wall's F-floors already exercise a single large DOM — the 'large' fixture is 357.2KB / 8808 nodes, and a full pipeline runs ~150ms (parse 6.6 / cascade 6.5 / layout 98 / dlist 6.4 / paint 30). So the claim 'F1/F2 don't cover a big single DOM' was itself stale: they do, at ~8.8k nodes, which brackets GitHub's 1k-5k-line blobs. THE SHAPE IS THE FINDING: layout is 65% of the pipeline and is the only super-linear term (mid 1208 nodes -> 13ms; large 8808 nodes -> 98ms, i.e. 7.3x nodes for 7.5x layout, so roughly LINEAR, not quadratic — no accidental O(n^2)). Remaining genuine unknown is not throughput but INTERACTIVITY on such a page (scroll/selection/re-layout after an edit), which F1/F2 do not model

receipt: tick 322: createLinearGradient/createRadialGradient now rasterize a REAL tiny-skia gradient shader (LinearGradient/RadialGradient two-point-conical) via __cvPathGradient — fill(), fillRect(), stroke(), strokeRect() all route to it; stops carry offsets, globalAlpha folds in, the shader is built at identity so the context transform locks it to the geometry. Honest limit: conic gradients keep the flat last-stop fallback (no SweepGradient wired), createPattern still null

what_breaks_without_it: hatch fills, textured/repeating backgrounds and tiled sprites set fillStyle=ctx.createPattern(img,'repeat'); createPattern returned null forever so the fill became null->black and every one painted a black block
receipt: tick 323: createPattern(img/canvas,'repeat'|'repeat-x'|'repeat-y'|'no-repeat') returns a real CanvasPattern that tiles the source through a tiny_skia Pattern shader via __cvPathPattern — fill(), fillRect(), stroke(), strokeRect() route to it, source pixels reuse the node-keyed drawImage registry (CANVASES/SOURCES, no new decode), globalAlpha folds in, built at identity so the context transform tiles it in user space. Honest limits: SpreadMode is not per-axis so repeat-x/y both tile and no-repeat pads its edges (not transparent); pattern.setTransform not wired

what_breaks_without_it: conic-gradient pie/donut chart fills, colour wheels, loading spinners and angular progress rings set fillStyle=ctx.createConicGradient(a,x,y); it fell back to a flat last-stop block

Completes the gradient story (linear t322 / radial t322 / conic t324 all real). fill/fillRect/stroke/strokeRect route to it; single/empty-stop still flat via color()

STILL MISSING (rung 2): WSOLA-class time-stretch so 1.5-2x stays AUDIBLE — the actual podcast use case; until then scaled playback is silent-and-honest.

receipt: tick 378 (taken one tick after the audit added it): scrollTo/scroll/scrollBy return Promise.resolve() — truthful, our scrolls are instant — AND the deeper pre-existing divergence fixed en route: __scrollTo now optimistically updates the page-visible SCROLL position, so `scrollTo(0,40); scrollY===40` holds synchronously as in a real browser (the host's application overwrites with the clamped truth; out-of-range over-reports only transiently). scrollp claim IN the wall; RED-proven both halves (promise removed → no-promise; optimistic update removed → continuation sees stale scrollY). scrollIntoView promise deferred (returns undefined per current spec — void — no change needed).

The intl probe RED-proves it with a de-DE assertion (1.234,5 — dot thousands, comma decimal) that only genuine ICU locale data yields; a stub or noicu build reports intl:no. what_breaks_without_it: a decorative full-bleed overlay (gradient scrim / drag-ghost / ::before sheen) swallows every click meant for the content beneath it — and an agent's elementFromPoint-based click-target resolution hits the transparent overlay, so the real button never fires
receipt: tick 448: pointer-events was dropped by the cascade entirely (getComputedStyle(el).pointerEvents undefined; elementFromPoint returned the topmost box even when pointer-events:none). Now (a) the inherited computed value is bridged from Stylo (servo build models auto/none only) into ComputedStyle, (b) a `none` node is dropped from the elementFromPoint candidate set via the published STYLES_PTR snapshot so the point passes through to the element behind, and (c) getComputedStyle surfaces pointerEvents + getPropertyValue('pointer-events'). Agentic actuation surface (component #2). RED-proven (efp returned the ghost overlay; getComputedStyle undefined). Caught a latent CSSOM enumeration-count bug (hardcoded 49→50) in the same edit. tick 449 COMPLETED the agentic half: the a11y-tree hit_test (the path the AGENT grounds a coordinate click through, distinct from JS elementFromPoint) now also passes through pointer-events:none — A11yNode carries a `hittable` flag set from Page::non_hittable_nodes() (mirrors invisible_nodes), the node stays in the tree (screen-reader announced) but drops out of hit_test. G_POINTER_EVENTS_A11Y, RED-proven (neutered skip → overlay wins).

what_breaks_without_it: bulk cursor-free reads; part of the Interop 2026 20%-weight set
receipt: tick 420: added getAllRecords(options) to BOTH the store and index shims — returns full {key, primaryKey, value} records in one call, honoring {query, count, direction} incl. nextunique/prevunique dedup. On the store key==primaryKey; on an index key=index key, primaryKey=store key (the RED-prover vs a getAll stand-in). Gated + RED-proven.

receipt: tick 434: both were undefined. label.control = the for= target (if labelable) else the first labelable descendant; control.labels = a static NodeList of every <label> whose .control resolves back (tree order, >1 allowed). Hidden input is non-labelable → .labels is null, <label for=hidden> claims nothing. Static NodeList, NOT the hot live() proxy (the childNodes UAF). RED-proven.

Self-contained builder in collections_js (NOT through the hot live() proxy — the childNodes UAF). RED-proven (pre-impl probe: type:undefined). KNOWN LIMIT: association by subtree, not the form= reassociation attribute. Direct form['name'] access still fails (needs a form-object proxy — separate).

RED-proven by removing the setAttribute wrap. (Property-reflector attribute writes e.g. id=/className= are a separate later edge.)

receipt: tick 437: input.form was undefined incl. the form= reassociation case (control OUTSIDE the <form>). The `sel.options.length = 0` clear-then-repopulate idiom now hits the DOM; indexed access/iteration/namedItem/add/remove pass through unchanged. RED-proven by unwrapping the Proxy.
